title: rubus × hemimalacus
intro: Rubus × hemimalacus, also known as the hybrid black raspberry or thimbleberry, is a type of bramble plant that belongs to the genus Rubus and is a cross between Rubus argutus and Rubus laciniatus. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rubus × hemimalacus include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and protect against cell damage. Rubus × hemimalacus can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and extracts, which are often used to support digestive health and boost the immune system.
